title: ggen Testing Definition of Done
subtitle: Comprehensive testing specification combining Chicago TDD, 80%+ coverage, mutation testing, and process mining validation
testing_mandate: Chicago TDD ONLY (zero mocks, real collaborators) with multi-surface corroboration (execution + observability + state + process + causality)

dimensions.name: Coverage Gates
dimensions.description: Minimum coverage thresholds for line coverage and mutation testing
dimensions.why_it_matters: Coverage metrics detect untested code paths, verify error handling, and ensure no silent failures. Mutation testing proves tests are detecting real bugs, not just passing vacuously.
success_criteria: Line coverage ≥80% across all workspace crates
Branch coverage ≥75% for critical paths (pipeline stages, error handlers)
Mutation score ≥60% (killed mutants / total mutants)
No crate with coverage drop from prior release
Error paths covered ≥85% (all Result branches tested)
Dead code identified and removed or flagged for future work
forbidden_patterns: Mocking code to artificially inflate coverage metrics
Test doubles that simulate real behavior instead of real I/O
Using `#[cfg(test)]` to hide unreachable code from coverage
Ignoring or skipping error paths to inflate pass rate
Fabricated test fixtures that don't cross system boundaries
Counting untested branches as 'exercised' without real failure cases
required_patterns: Real I/O boundaries (filesystem, HTTP, database) in test collaborators
Error case testing with actual error conditions (missing files, network failure)
Mutation testing with cargo-mutants to verify test sensitivity
Coverage reports from `cargo tarpaulin` or similar real instrumentation
Explicit test cases for each error type in Result enums
Saturation testing: tests fail when mutation deletes critical logic
commands: cargo make test -- --test-threads=1 (for race condition detection)
validation_checklist: [ ] Line coverage report generated and ≥80% per crate
[ ] Mutation score ≥60% confirmed via cargo mutants
[ ] All error enum variants covered by at least one test case
[ ] No dead code reported (or dead code justified + documented)
[ ] Coverage trend: no regression from prior release
[ ] Mutation killing rate > 60% (real test sensitivity)

dimensions.name: Chicago TDD Enforcement
dimensions.description: Zero mocks, zero test doubles. All tests use real collaborators and verify observable state changes.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Mocks hide integration bugs and create a false sense of correctness. Chicago TDD forces tests to cross system boundaries, proving the code actually works with real dependencies.
success_criteria: Zero imports of mockall, mockito, or similar mock libraries
Zero Mock* structs defined in test code
Zero .expect_*() / .times() / behavior verification chains
All external collaborators (HTTP, DB, filesystem) are real
All tests verify observable state (files exist, DB rows written, HTTP responses)
No test doubles for 'isolation' or 'testability'
forbidden_patterns: use mockall::mock! or #[mockall::automock]
let mock_client = MockHttpClient::new()
mock_client.expect_get().times(1).returning(...)
struct FakeDatabase { ... } (test double avoiding real DB)
struct InMemoryStorage { ... } (fake simulating real storage)
Traits defined for DI (dependency injection) just for testability
assert_eq!(mock.call_count(...), 1) (behavior verification)
Mocking observability: @patch('get_tracer') in tests
Fabricating OCEL events or span dicts by hand
required_patterns: Real reqwest::Client for HTTP tests
Real SqlitePool or PostgreSQL for database tests
Real std::fs operations or tempfile::TempDir for filesystem
Real environment variables (std::env)
Real process execution (std::process::Command)
Real TempDir from tempfile crate (not isolation, real I/O)
Real OTel spans captured via Tempo/Jaeger clients
State assertions: assert!(file.exists()), assert_eq!(db_row.count(), 1)
Timeout-based concurrency tests (not mock-based timing)
validation_checklist: [ ] grep mockall returns zero results (except archived tests)
[ ] No Mock* structs in crates/*/tests/ directories
[ ] No .expect_*() or .times() chains in test code
[ ] All test collaborators use real I/O (verified by code inspection)
[ ] Tests fail when real boundary is broken (e.g., delete pack file → test fails)
[ ] Integration tests cross ≥1 system boundary (DB/HTTP/FS)

dimensions.name: Test Categorization
dimensions.description: Classify tests by type and ensure each category is comprehensive and purposeful
dimensions.why_it_matters: Different test types exercise different failure modes. Unit tests catch logic bugs. Integration tests catch wiring bugs. Property tests catch edge cases. Absence of a category signals incomplete coverage.
success_criteria: Unit tests: all public functions tested in isolation (real collaborators mocked at module boundary only)
Integration tests: cross-crate workflows with real I/O
Property tests: edge cases, fuzz testing, invariant checking
Snapshot tests: deterministic output (generated code, receipts)
Security tests: tamper detection, privilege escalation, injection
Determinism tests: RNG_SEED=42 reproducibility
Performance tests: SLO validation via criterion benchmarks
Sabotage tests: negative paths proving system fails loudly
forbidden_patterns: Tests with names like 'test_thing' without describing what they verify
Tests that only assert happy path (no error cases)
Tests that assume sequential execution without synchronization
Tests that depend on execution order (not isolated)
Tests that use global state without cleanup
Mock-based unit tests (defeats purpose of isolation verification)
Parameterized tests without clear invariant under test
required_patterns: Unit tests: test one public function per test, assert on return value + state
Integration tests: #[tokio::test] for async, real DB/HTTP/FS, clear arrange/act/assert
Property tests: #[cfg(test)] fn prop_* using proptest!, generates 1000s of cases
Snapshot tests: insta::assert_snapshot!(generated_code) for reproducibility
Security tests: tamper lockfile/receipt, verify hard failure
Determinism tests: set RNG_SEED=42, run 10x, verify identical output
Performance tests: criterion::black_box(), measure latency/throughput, fail if SLO violated
Sabotage tests: delete files, corrupt state, verify error (not silent failure)
commands: RNG_SEED=42 cargo test --workspace (determinism: run same seed 3x, diff output)

unit.scope: Single function, pure logic, minimal external calls
unit.collaborators: None (or real at crate boundary only)

integration.scope: Cross-crate workflows, real I/O, async operations
integration.collaborators: Real filesystem, real database, real HTTP
integration.execution: Async with real I/O, <30s per test
integration.assertion: Observable state (files, DB rows, HTTP responses)

property.scope: Invariants that hold for all inputs, edge case discovery
property.assertion: Invariant holds for all generated inputs

snapshot.collaborators: Real ontology input, real Tera renderer
snapshot.execution: Synchronous, produce output once per schema change
snapshot.assertion: Output matches golden snapshot via insta

security.scope: Tamper detection, privilege escalation, injection attacks
security.collaborators: Real files, real signatures, real validation logic

determinism.scope: Reproducibility across runs with fixed seed
determinism.collaborators: Same as unit/integration but with RNG_SEED=42
determinism.execution: Run 3x with same seed, verify identical output
determinism.assertion: Output hash matches across all runs

performance.scope: SLO validation, latency bounds, throughput targets
performance.assertion: Throughput/latency within SLO, fail if exceeded

sabotage.scope: Negative paths, error handling, fail-fast verification
sabotage.assertion: Error returned or exit code non-zero

validation_checklist: [ ] ≥1 unit test per public function
[ ] ≥1 integration test per crate crossing ≥1 boundary
[ ] ≥1 property test for data structures (no edge cases missed)
[ ] ≥1 snapshot test for deterministic code generation
[ ] ≥1 security test for each security boundary (tamper detection, authorization)
[ ] ≥1 determinism test per crate with RNG_SEED=42
[ ] ≥1 performance test per critical path (SLO validation)
[ ] ≥2 sabotage tests per error type (missing resource, corrupt state)

dimensions.name: Error Path Testing
dimensions.description: All error types exercised, all edge cases covered, all Result branches tested
dimensions.why_it_matters: Untested error paths are silent failures. Tests must verify each error type is returned correctly and handled loudly (no swallowing errors).
success_criteria: Every error enum variant tested (at least once)
Every Result::Err branch executes and asserts correct error type
Every error message is non-generic (specific to failure mode)
Resource cleanup verified on error (files deleted, DB rolled back)
Cascading errors traced (root cause → intermediate → surface)
Timeout paths tested (deadlocks, infinite loops detected)
Panic paths eliminated from production code
forbidden_patterns: unwrap() or expect() in production code (only tests allowed)
Errors logged but not returned (fail-open pattern)
Generic error messages ('Error: operation failed')
Swallowing errors with || None patterns
Error paths not tested (Err arm is unreachable in tests)
Panics in production code (must return Result)
Timeout not set (infinite loops possible)
required_patterns: Every error::* variant tested with explicit error creation
Test asserts error type via pattern matching or .is_err() + message check
Error messages include context (what failed + why + suggested fix)
Resource cleanup tested (temp files deleted, DB transactions rolled back)
Cascading error tests (error propagates correctly through call stack)
Timeout tests for potential deadlocks or infinite loops
No panics in library code (only Result<T, E>)
Error variants use descriptive names (PackNotFound not PackError)
validation_checklist: [ ] All error enum variants appear in at least one test
[ ] All Result::Err assertions use pattern matching (not generic .is_err())
[ ] All error messages are specific and actionable
[ ] All resource cleanup verified on error (temp files deleted, etc.)
[ ] All cascading errors traced through call stack
[ ] No unwrap() in production src/ (only tests/)
[ ] No panics in production code
[ ] Timeout set for all potentially blocking operations

error_testing_patterns.single_error: Test one error condition, assert correct error type and message
error_testing_patterns.cascading_errors: Call stack A→B→C, error at C propagates through B to A, verify at surface
error_testing_patterns.resource_cleanup: Error during operation, verify resources freed (temp files, DB transactions)
error_testing_patterns.timeout_detection: Set timeout shorter than operation, verify timeout error not hang
error_testing_patterns.error_recovery: Error occurs, operation restarts cleanly, no state corruption

dimensions.name: Concurrency Testing
dimensions.description: Race conditions, deadlocks, and concurrent state mutation validated
dimensions.why_it_matters: Concurrency bugs manifest intermittently and are hard to debug. Tests must be deterministic and detect races, deadlocks, and data corruption under concurrent load.
success_criteria: All shared state protected by Arc<Mutex<T>> or channels
No raw pointers or unsafe { } without explicit safety justification
Deterministic concurrency tests with RNG_SEED=42
Race condition detection via ThreadSanitizer (TSAN) or similar
Deadlock detection via timeout + panic on hang
Load tests with ≥10x expected concurrent operations
Async tests properly synchronized (await all tasks)
forbidden_patterns: Data races: shared mutable state without synchronization
Deadlocks: circular lock acquisition without timeout
Lost updates: concurrent writes without locking
Use-after-free: returning references to stack-allocated data
Unsafe code without safety comments + examples
Non-deterministic concurrency (order of tasks undefined)
Async tests that don't await all tasks before asserting
required_patterns: All shared state in Arc<Mutex<T>> or Arc<RwLock<T>>
Timeouts on all lock acquisitions (detect deadlocks)
Deterministic task ordering via tokio::spawn + join_all
Race condition tests: spawn N concurrent tasks, verify consistent result
Deadlock tests: acquire locks in different orders, verify no hang
Load tests: ≥10 concurrent operations, verify no data corruption
Sync primitives: channels for async communication, not shared mutable state
Async tests: #[tokio::test] with proper .await and task joining
commands: cargo test --workspace --release (race conditions less likely but not guaranteed)
grep -r 'Arc<Mutex' crates/ --include='*.rs' (verify shared state is locked)
validation_checklist: [ ] TSAN passes (no data races reported)
[ ] Deadlock timeout tests pass (no hangs)
[ ] Load tests pass with ≥10x concurrent operations
[ ] Deterministic concurrency tests pass with RNG_SEED=42
[ ] No unsafe code except where explicitly justified
[ ] All async tests properly await all tasks
[ ] All shared state protected by Arc<Mutex<T>>
[ ] Race condition tests demonstrate consistent results under load

concurrency_test_scenarios.read_under_load: Multiple readers, one writer, verify reads see consistent state
concurrency_test_scenarios.write_ordering: Multiple writers to same resource, verify no lost updates
concurrency_test_scenarios.task_cancellation: Cancel task mid-operation, verify cleanup + no leak
concurrency_test_scenarios.channel_capacity: Send N messages through bounded channel, verify backpressure
concurrency_test_scenarios.lock_timeout: Acquire lock with timeout, verify timeout not deadlock

dimensions.name: Sabotage Tests
dimensions.description: Negative path tests proving system fails loudly on invalid state (not silently)
dimensions.why_it_matters: Systems must detect and reject invalid state immediately. Sabotage tests verify fail-fast behavior: corrupted lockfiles, missing packs, tampered receipts all cause hard errors (exit 1), not silent failures or warnings.
success_criteria: Missing pack file → ggen sync --locked exits non-zero
Corrupted lockfile → ggen sync exits non-zero with error message
Corrupted receipt signature → ggen receipt verify returns is_valid: false
Missing verification key → ggen receipt verify fails (not silent success)
Tampered artifact hash → receipt verification fails
Impossible state injection detected → validation rejects
All negative tests cause hard exit (1), never silent failure
forbidden_patterns: Errors logged with warn! but operation continues (fail-open)
Silent return of None instead of error
Corruption detected but ignored (|| default handling)
Error cases not tested at all
Mocking the validator to test 'failure handling'
Using || true to suppress error signals
Skipping validation gates to avoid negative test setup
required_patterns: Each sabotage test corrupts one aspect of system state
Corruption detected before any artifacts are written
Error message is specific (not generic)
Exit code is non-zero for all failure cases
Cleanup happens even on error (temp files removed, DB rolled back)
Sabotage cannot be undone silently (must restart from clean state)
Tests fail if sabotage does NOT trigger error (proves validation works)
commands: rm .ggen/packs.lock && ggen sync --locked && echo 'FAIL: should not succeed'
echo '{}' > .ggen/packs.lock && ggen sync && echo 'FAIL: should not succeed'
validation_checklist: [ ] Missing pack → exit non-zero
[ ] Corrupted lockfile → exit non-zero
[ ] Tampered receipt → is_valid: false
[ ] Missing key → error (not silent success)
[ ] Hash mismatch → verification fails
[ ] All negative tests use real state corruption (not mocks)
[ ] Error messages are specific and actionable
[ ] No silent failures (all errors surface as exit codes)

sabotage_test_scenarios.missing_resource: Pack file deleted after install, ggen sync --locked fails
sabotage_test_scenarios.corrupt_manifest: Lockfile contains invalid JSON, ggen sync fails
sabotage_test_scenarios.hash_mismatch: Pack file changed, digest doesn't match, sync fails
sabotage_test_scenarios.impossible_state: Receipt before digest, wrong state order, validation fails
sabotage_test_scenarios.missing_key: Verification key deleted, receipt verify fails

dimensions.name: AAA Pattern Enforcement
dimensions.description: Every test follows Arrange → Act → Assert with real state changes
dimensions.why_it_matters: AAA pattern makes tests readable, maintainable, and forces clear separation between setup, action, and verification. Violating AAA leads to unclear tests that conflate setup with assertion.
success_criteria: Every test has 3 sections: Arrange (setup), Act (action), Assert (verify)
Arrange creates real objects, real data (not mocks)
Act calls one public function under test
Assert verifies observable state (not internal state)
No setup in Act (all initialization in Arrange)
No assertions in Arrange (all verification in Assert)
Each test is independent (no dependency on other tests' state)
forbidden_patterns: Assertions in Arrange section
Setup/initialization in Act section
Tests that depend on execution order
Global state mutations (e.g., static mut)
Multiple unrelated assertions per test (one concern per test)
Comments like 'TODO: add assert' (incomplete tests)
Mocking in Arrange (defeats the purpose of isolation)
required_patterns: Arrange: create real objects (TempDir, SqlitePool, reqwest::Client)
Act: call one public function, capture result
Assert: verify observable state (files exist, DB rows, return value)
Comments separating sections: // Arrange // Act // Assert
One concern per test (one reason to fail)
Independent tests (setUp/tearDown for cleanup)
Descriptive test names indicating what is being verified
commands: grep -c '//' crates/*/tests/*.rs (rough gauge of comment density)
validation_checklist: [ ] All tests have clear Arrange/Act/Assert sections
[ ] No assertions in Arrange
[ ] No setup in Act
[ ] Arrange creates real objects (not mocks)
[ ] Act calls exactly one public function
[ ] Assert verifies observable state (external proof)
[ ] Tests are independent (no cross-test state)
[ ] Test names describe what is verified (test_sync_with_missing_pack_fails)

dimensions.name: Cross-Crate Testing
dimensions.description: Integration across workspace members validated, cyclic dependencies prevented
dimensions.why_it_matters: 15 crates in the workspace; each crate-to-crate boundary is a potential integration bug. Tests must validate workflows spanning multiple crates and verify no cyclic dependencies.
success_criteria: Integration tests for each crate boundary crossing
No cyclic crate dependencies (verified by cargo tree)
Public API tested from dependent crates (not just unit tests within crate)
Error propagation tested across crate boundaries
Type re-exports are public and tested
All feature flags tested in combination
Workspace members compile independently (single crate at a time)
forbidden_patterns: Cyclic dependencies (A depends on B, B depends on A)
Private functions exposed for testing (pub(crate) workarounds)
Integration tests inside crate (should be in separate test crate or integration tests/)
Feature flags not tested in CI (silent breaks when feature disabled)
Missing or incomplete Cargo.toml dependencies (using private paths)
required_patterns: Integration tests for each crate boundary (ggen-core → ggen-cli → ggen-lsp)
Cross-crate workflows tested end-to-end (pack resolution → sync → receipt verification)
Public API of each crate tested from dependent crate
Error types and messages consistent across crates
Type re-exports in lib.rs are tested (pub use path::Type)
Feature combinations tested in CI (e.g., --features feature1,feature2)
Workspace members compile independently: cargo build -p ggen-core
commands: cargo tree --workspace (verify no cycles, visualize dependencies)
cargo build -p ggen-core && cargo build -p ggen-cli (single crate builds)
validation_checklist: [ ] cargo tree shows no cycles
[ ] Each crate compiles independently (cargo build -p <crate>)
[ ] Integration tests for each crate boundary exist
[ ] Public API of each crate tested from dependent
[ ] Error types consistent across crate boundaries
[ ] Type re-exports are tested (lib.rs pub use statements)
[ ] Feature flags tested in CI
[ ] Workspace compiles with all features enabled

cross_crate_test_scenarios.pack_resolution: ggen-marketplace resolves pack, ggen-core consumes it, ggen-cli renders output
cross_crate_test_scenarios.receipt_chain: ggen-core emits receipt, ggen-graph validates chain, ggen-cli verifies signature
cross_crate_test_scenarios.error_propagation: Error from ggen-core propagates through ggen-cli to user (error message preserved)
cross_crate_test_scenarios.configuration: ggen-config parses TOML, ggen-core consumes config, ggen-cli displays results
cross_crate_test_scenarios.sync_workflow: Full sync: ggen-config → ggen-marketplace → ggen-core → ggen-graph → receipt emission

before_commit: [ ] Chicago TDD: zero mockall imports, all tests use real collaborators
[ ] Test categorization: unit + integration + property + snapshot + security + determinism + performance + sabotage
[ ] Error paths: all error enum variants tested, all Result branches covered
[ ] Concurrency: no data races (TSAN), no deadlocks (timeout tests), deterministic (RNG_SEED=42)
[ ] Sabotage tests: missing pack fails, corrupt lockfile fails, tampered receipt detected
[ ] AAA pattern: every test has Arrange/Act/Assert, no assertions in Arrange
[ ] Cross-crate: integration tests for crate boundaries, no cycles, independent builds
validation_commands: cargo make timeout-check    # Verify timeout command exists
cargo make check            # No compiler errors
cargo make test             # All tests pass
cargo make lint             # No clippy warnings
cargo make slo-check        # Performance SLOs met
RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=thread' cargo test             # TSAN race detection
grep -r 'unwrap\|expect' crates/*/src/ --include='*.rs' # No panics in production

multi_surface_corroboration.doctrine: A claim is proven only if it appears in at least 3 of these 5 surfaces: execution + observability + state + process + causality

surfaces.execution: Code actually runs, function returns normally
surfaces.observability: OTel spans emitted with correct attributes (requires RUST_LOG=trace)
surfaces.state: Observable state changed (files, DB rows, return values)
surfaces.process: OCEL event log records the transition (process mining validation)
surfaces.causality: Preceding events linked to this event via receipt chain

multi_surface_corroboration.example_multiface_test: Testing that an operator ran successfully requires proof across 4+ surfaces: (1) operator executes (2) OTel span exists (3) artifact state changed (4) receipt links prior to new artifact

otel_validation.mandate: For any feature touching external services (LLM, MCP, HTTP, database), OTEL spans are required proof. Tests passing is insufficient.
otel_validation.verification_procedure: export RUST_LOG=trace,ggen_ai=trace; cargo test -p <crate> --test <test> -- --nocapture 2>&1 | grep -E '(llm\.|mcp\.|pipeline\.|quality_gate)'; verify all required spans and attributes present

anti_cheating_enforcement.principle: The easiest path to passing tests must be real execution. Faking must be harder than the real thing.
anti_cheating_enforcement.no_mocking_observability: Cannot @patch get_tracer(); must use real Tempo/Jaeger client
anti_cheating_enforcement.no_fabricated_fixtures: Cannot fabricate OCEL events or span dicts; must extract from real traces
anti_cheating_enforcement.evidence_must_be_externalizable: Assertion must be verifiable outside the test (files, DB, OTel)
anti_cheating_enforcement.causality_not_assertions: Cannot just assert state == BRED; must prove causality via traces and receipts
anti_cheating_enforcement.no_test_naming_loopholes: Cannot create tests/chicago_tdd/ tier or @mark.chicago_tdd; enforce via behavior
anti_cheating_enforcement.impossible_state_injection: Tests that fabricate impossible state (wrong receipt chain) must be rejected by validator
anti_cheating_enforcement.resilience_to_agent_laziness: Lazy agent cannot pass test with minimal code; must cross real boundary

forbidden_anti_patterns: London TDD: mockall, Mock structs, behavior verification (.times(), .expect_)
Fail-open errors: warn!() instead of Err()
Unforgeable assertions: assert!(my_function_called) with no external evidence
Fabricated observability: @patch('get_tracer') or hand-constructed span dicts
Dead code: unreachable branches, #[cfg(test)] hiding code from coverage
Test doubles: FakeDatabase, InMemoryStorage, struct Fake*
Panics in production: unwrap(), expect(), panic!() outside tests
Silent failures: || None, || default, swallowing errors
Cyclic dependencies: crate A depends on B, B depends on A
Mocking the validator to hide cheating: mocking SHACL gate, mocking receipt verification
references: .claude/rules/rust/testing.md — Chicago TDD mandate and test types
.claude/rules/otel-validation.md — OTEL span verification for external services
.claude/rules/coding-agent-mistakes.md — 6-question patch contract, invariant definitions, sabotage tests
justfile — just check, just test, just lint, just slo-check

version_history.changes: Initial Definition of Done: 8 dimensions, multi-surface corroboration, Chicago TDD + coverage + mutation + OTEL validation
